Unclosed tag" # Should not match as the tag is not properly closed match_invalid = HTML_TAG_ONLY_RE.match(invalid_html_input) assert match_invalid is None, "Expected not to match an invalid HTML tag!" # Test input with multiple lines multi_line_html_input = """ Title
Hello World!
""" # Should match the first tag across multiple lines match_multi_line = HTML_TAG_ONLY_RE.findall(multi_line_html_input) assert len(match_multi_line) > 0, "Expected to find multiple HTML tags in multi-line input!" ``` This test case includes assertions to ensure that: - Valid HTML tags are matched. - Invalid HTML is not matched. - The regex works correctly with multi-line strings, taking advantage of the `re.DOTALL` functionality. When executed against the original code, all assertions should pass. However, when executed against the mutant code where `re.DOTALL` has been improperly modified, the test will fail on multi-line or improperly formatted HTML inputs.
